title: ATDD acceptance suite fails on every scenario (SQLITE_IOERR)
evidence: tests/acceptance.test.js:20-26 beforeAll requires src/server (which opens the books.db handle at server.js:15) then fs.unlinkSync deletes that same file; async table creation/queries then hit the unlinked inode. _agent_stdout.log:7970-7974 shows 'FAIL tests/acceptance.test.js ... SQLITE_IOERR: disk I/O error' and 11 failed / 1 passed, every endpoint returning 500.
suggestion: Give each run a fresh DB before the server opens it: set a per-test DB path (e.g. :memory: or a tmp file) via env before require, or delete the file before importing the server, not after.

title: Acceptance scenarios are not atomic/independent (ATDD prompt violated)
evidence: tests/acceptance.test.js deletes books.db only once in beforeAll (line 22) with no per-test reset; 'should list all books' asserts toHaveLength(2) (line 130) yet earlier tests already persisted rows, and ISBN 123-456-789 is reused across the filter and duplicate-ISBN tests. The ATDD prompt requires each scenario to 'start from a running but empty service and share no data with other tests'.
suggestion: Reset the datastore in beforeEach (truncate the books table or recreate an in-memory DB) so every scenario starts empty; stop hard-coding collection lengths that assume global state.

title: language=typescript run delivered plain JavaScript (idiomatic=0.0)
evidence: Run factor language=typescript (stack.json) but `find . -name '*.ts'` = 0; src/server.js:1 and src/database.js:1 use CommonJS `require(...)` with no type annotations, tsconfig, or build step. TASK.md constraint 'Use the specified language'. retort scored idiomatic=0.0 (scores.json).
suggestion: Author the service in TypeScript (typed Express handlers, ts-jest or compiled build) or drop the typescript factor for this agent; a TS deliverable with zero TS does not satisfy the language constraint.

title: Tautological placeholder test verifies nothing
evidence: tests/unit.test.js:19 asserts `expect(true).toBe(true)` under 'should have the required endpoints', with a comment calling it 'just a placeholder test to satisfy the structure'. It passes unconditionally and exercises no code.
suggestion: Replace with a real assertion against a route handler, or delete it so it does not inflate the passing-test count.

title: Soft-skipped health test swallows failures
evidence: tests/simple.test.js:26-34 wraps the /health request in try/catch and on error only console.logs 'Skipping health check test due to setup issues' with no assertion, so the test can never fail even if /health is broken.
suggestion: Remove the try/catch (let the assertion throw) so the test actually gates behavior.

title: functional.test.js validation-grep assertion fails (buggy regex, not a code defect)
evidence: tests/functional.test.js:23 `expect(serverContent).toMatch(/title.*author.*required/)`; server.js emits 'Title and author are required' (capital T) so the lowercase, single-line regex never matches. _agent_stdout.log:12278-12282 shows FAIL tests/functional.test.js, 1 failed / 2 passed on the agent's final test run.
suggestion: Fix the assertion (case-insensitive, or assert behavior via a 400 response) — the underlying validation in server.js:46 is correct; the test is wrong.

title: src/database.js is dead code duplicating the schema
evidence: src/database.js exports {db, dbPath} and creates the books table, but server.js:12-30 opens its own sqlite3.Database and defines the same schema; nothing imports database.js.
suggestion: Delete src/database.js or refactor server.js to consume it, so the schema has a single source of truth.

title: ISBN uniqueness enforced beyond the spec (returns 400, not 409)
evidence: server.js:57-61 maps a UNIQUE isbn violation to HTTP 400 'ISBN must be unique'; the task spec (TASK.md) requires validation only for title/author and does not mention ISBN uniqueness.
suggestion: Fine as an enhancement; if kept, 409 Conflict is the more conventional status for a duplicate-resource collision.
